retail work
The first lessons were not about circuits. They were about showing up, speaking clearly, handling small responsibilities, and noticing how operations survive through repeatable habits.
customer service
Document work taught me that trust is built through accuracy. A small typo, a missed verification step, or a rushed explanation can become someone else's problem.
engineering education
ECE gave the curiosity a direction. Digital electronics, mathematics, signals, devices, and systems started becoming connected instead of separate subjects.
learning programming
C and basic tooling made me understand that logic has to be expressed precisely. The machine only does what was actually written.
discovering digital design
Flip-flops, gates, timing, and state machines made hardware feel programmable without becoming software. That boundary became interesting.
discovering vlsi
VLSI turned the question deeper: how does a design become silicon, and what does it mean to create something that exists below the surface of every modern system?
learning matlab
MATLAB became a place to test signals, models, and mathematical ideas before trying to force them into hardware or physical intuition.
learning simulation tools
Simulation made the invisible visible. It also taught me that a clean waveform can still hide a wrong assumption.
building projects
Projects are where the theory becomes honest. They expose gaps faster than reading alone.
documenting everything
Documentation turns one struggle into reusable evidence. It lets future me see what past me misunderstood.
becoming an engineer
The direction is simple: keep building, keep breaking, keep explaining, and become someone who can move an idea from imagination to reality.
